summary_of_threads_started_this_week:
The recent discussions within the Bitcoin development community encompass a range of topics aimed at enhancing the protocol's efficiency, security, and functionality. A notable proposal addresses performance issues in the Silent Payments module by suggesting a "K_max" protocol limit to mitigate the impact of transactions with a large number of recipients, a change documented in a draft for a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) available for review [here](https://github.com/theStack/bips/commit/961d1442139ceecd6c0cc5775ef911d69aabed4c). Meanwhile, advancements in the bitcoin backbone have been introduced, including support for compact-block BIP152 and improvements in transaction synchronization, with detailed discussions about further enhancements exploring interface extensions for blockchain data interaction, as shared on [bitcoinbackbone.org](http://bitcoinbackbone.org).

In academic contributions, a paper titled ["Market Power and the Bitcoin Protocol"](http://ewfs.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/tx_fees-18.pdf) challenges the conventional wisdom regarding Bitcoin miners' behavior by suggesting that miners may strategically forego certain transaction fees to influence average fees over time, indicating a complex economic interplay within the network. Concurrently, a conceptual exploration of Bitcoin's transaction selection and block assembly processes highlights the intricate economic incentives and marginal trade-offs miners navigate, suggesting an optimization logic that significantly affects transaction inclusion and block utilization, as detailed in discussions found at [delvingbitcoin.org](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/a-max-plus-perspective-on-package-relay-and-block-assembly/2235).

Additionally, the introduction of Boomerang, a novel Bitcoin cold storage protocol, aims to enhance security and resistance to coercion without altering consensus mechanisms, emphasizing a non-deterministic withdrawal process detailed [here](https://github.com/bitryonix/boomerang_design). Lastly, the release of Bitcoin Inquisition version 29.2 incorporates several proposed consensus changes, including support for BIP 118 and BIP 119, aiming to refine protocol functionality, with further information available on [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin-inquisition/bitcoin/releases/tag/v29.2-inq). These discussions and developments reflect a dynamic and evolving ecosystem focused on improving the robustness and capabilities of the Bitcoin network.

new_threads_this_week.title: BIP-352: Limiting the number of per-group recipients (K_max)
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- The BIP-352 scanning method for libsecp256k1 has performance issues with adversarial transactions.
- A new "K_max" protocol limit is proposed to address recipient overload in transactions.
- Community feedback is sought via GitHub on this protocol change to enhance Silent Payments.

new_threads_this_week.title: New bitcoin backbone code release: BIP152, outbound tx support + libbitcoinkernel API feedbacks
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- The recent update improves bitcoin backbone, focusing on compact-block BIP152 and synchronization.
- A new custom C interface enhances interaction with consensus code, offering flexibility.
- Developers suggest interface extensions for better blockchain data interaction and mempool management.

new_threads_this_week.title: Lehar / Parlour Paper
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- The paper argues Bitcoin miners don't always maximize fees, suggesting strategic behavior for future gains.
- Its findings challenge the notion of miners solely seeking immediate profit, indicating complex economic strategies.
- The debate focuses on the paper's empirical evidence and its impact on understanding miner incentives.

new_threads_this_week.title: A max-plus perspective on package relay and block assembly
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Complex interplay in Bitcoin affects transaction inclusion and block utilization.
- Miners aim to maximize fees within block weight limits, using optimization strategies.
- Understanding optimization in Bitcoin is vital for assessing miner behavior and protocol navigation.

new_threads_this_week.title: Boomerang: Bitcoin Cold Storage with Built-In Duress Protection
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Boomerang enhances Bitcoin cold storage with protocol-level duress protection, improving physical security.
- It features a non-deterministic withdrawal process, and compatibility with Bitcoin consensus, without requiring consensus changes.
- The protocol provides a proof-of-concept in Rust, with technical details and collaboration invitations on GitHub.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Inqusition 29.2
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Inquisition version 29.2 introduces significant updates including BIP 118 and BIP 119.
- It also integrates BIN-24 series and BIP 54, enhancing protocol functionality and security.
- New features require direct connection to inquisition nodes for transaction relay.

active_posts_this_week.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Antoine explains constraining nSequence ensures correct timelock validation in Bitcoin BIP.
- This constraint prevents duplicate transactions, ensuring unique transaction IDs post-BIP 34.
- He addresses critique and suggests expanding documentation to clarify enforcement from genesis block.

active_posts_this_week.title: Benchmarking Bitcoin Script Evaluation for the Varops Budget (Great Script Restoration)
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- New opcodes enhance Bitcoin's scripting for efficient cryptographic applications.
- The [OP_CAT based zkp verifier](https://bitcoinmagazine.com/technical/a-zero-knowledge-proof-is-verified-on-bitcoin-for-the-first-time-in-history) is a groundbreaking, lightweight implementation.
- Future exploration into opcodes like OP_MUL could further optimize blockchain computations.

active_posts_this_week.title: Disposing of "dust attack" UTXOs
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email compares Dandelion and private broadcast methods for cryptocurrency privacy.
- It questions the reliability of Tor/I2P and Dandelion's effectiveness against DoS attacks.
- It highlights the complexities in achieving transaction privacy in cryptocurrencies.

active_posts_this_week.title: OP_CC: A simple introspection opcode to enable cheaper consolidations
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Smaller blocks may reduce privacy by discouraging techniques that obscure transactions.
- Larger blocks could enhance privacy but may lead to less efficient resource use.
- The trade-off between block size and privacy highlights the complexity of optimizing blockchain.

active_posts_this_week.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The importance of naming the Bitcoin Improvement Proposal was discussed, emphasizing community input.
- Acknowledgement was given to individuals' contributions to spend script optionality in the proposal.
- An inquiry about concerns beyond the name and spending rules opened dialogue for further refinement.

active_posts_this_week.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Utilizing a single recovery key for multiple digital signatures simplifies management while ensuring security.
- The approach balances convenience with privacy concerns, mitigating risks through infrequent use.
- It offers a cost-effective solution for blockchain inefficiencies while prioritizing user privacy.

active_posts_this_week.title: BLISK: Boolean circuit Logic Integrated into the Single Key
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email discusses converting complex AND/OR sequences to CNF, especially including NOT gates' handling.
- It notes challenges in applying CNF in systems without NOT gate equivalents, like Bitcoin.
- The conversation also explores simplifying multisignature protocols by abstracting Boolean gate policies.

active_posts_this_week.title: Would OP_SUCCESS (OP_CAT) be spent?
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Upgrade hooks facilitate safer Bitcoin network upgrades, minimizing risk and enhancing security.
- Soft forks modify consensus rules, emphasizing Bitcoin's decentralized decision-making process.
- The approach to Bitcoin governance must be robust and flexible, securing broad community consensus.

active_posts_this_week.title: Bithoven: A Formally Verified, Imperative Smart Contract Language for Bitcoin
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Bithoven prioritizes strong static analysis over expressiveness in its design philosophy.
- Its approach limits support for applications resembling covenants.
- This focus ensures robust analysis capabilities, even at the expense of functionality.

active_posts_this_week.title: Bitcoin as Digital Latin: Asymptotic Ossification and the End of Semantic Drift
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin's halvening events impact blockchain's stability, similar to the necessity of DNA stability.
- Changes at Bitcoin's foundational level risk causing chainsplits detrimental to Lightning Network operators.
- Resistance from LN node operators against non-beneficial changes stresses their influence on network stability.
